description: Produce a source-backed gap list between the current Windows Electron island and the macOS Phase 7 behavior so later parity tasks have exact scope.
steps: Read docs/windows-parity-contract.md and the macOS Phase 7 implementation summaries on master (agent-state.md, codex-progress.md at the branch point) to enumerate candidate gaps.

For each candidate gap, cite the Windows code path (DynamicIslandWidget.tsx / main.cjs) and the mac behavior it diverges from: login-free music default, Advanced Features toggle, login-required square, island update prompt, island download activity, Reduce Motion handling.
Classify each gap as back-port, intentional Windows divergence, or not-applicable (e.g. notch-specific geometry), with a one-line rationale.
Write the result to docs/windows-parity-gap.md with per-gap acceptance criteria, and update the task scopes below if the audit contradicts them.

description: Run the Windows island as a login-free music-only widget by default, matching the macOS basic mode.
steps: Remove the logged-out '点击登录' compact presentation as the default entry; the logged-out compact island stays visually quiet and music takeover works without any session.
Allow music takeover, expanded music card, and playback controls with no auth session; review/todo/reminder features remain hidden while logged out.
Persist the mode through the existing Electron config (app-config.json) so relaunch restores the same presentation.
Verify logged-out launch shows no login UI and issues no protected API requests, music takeover and release still work, and logged-in behavior is unchanged.

description: Add an Advanced Features toggle that gates auth, review, todo, and reminder work, matching the macOS capability policy.
steps: Add an Advanced Features setting (default disabled) surfaced from the tray menu and persisted in app-config.json; login entry appears only while enabled.
When disabled: skip session verification, stop review/todo/reminder pollers, hide protected interaction paths, and preserve any stored session without sending logout.
When enabled: reuse the existing browser login + memoryflow://callback flow, token storage, and polling (GET /widget/summary every 30s, GET /todos/... every 60s); do not duplicate service logic.
Verify disabled launch, enable-then-login, disable-preserves-session, and re-enable-restores-session lifecycles with no duplicate timers or protected requests while disabled.

description: Show the square Login Required presentation when an Advanced-enabled logged-out user clicks the island, matching the macOS Phase 7 presentation adapted to a notch-less screen.
steps: Add a loginRequired presentation to DynamicIslandWidget.tsx: expanded visible width equals height, anchored top-center, entered through the existing shell spring (stiffness 280, damping 30, mass 1.2).

Render only the localized four-character Simplified Chinese Login Required message and route taps into this presentation only when Advanced Features is enabled and auth is logged out; browser login stays reachable from the tray/settings entry.
Support outside-click collapse, repeated taps, music takeover and return, and reduced-motion rendering.
Verify square geometry, tap routing, collapse behavior, and that basic (Advanced-disabled) mode never shows this presentation.

description: Move the update experience into the island: a square prompt with Update/Later capsules and a download activity with left spinner and right percentage, replacing the separate data-URL update windows.
steps: Bridge electron-updater states (checking, available, download progress, ready, failed) from main.cjs to the widget over IPC as typed events; keep region-aware feed selection (GitHub + gh-proxy mirrors) unchanged in the main process.

Reuse the login-required square geometry for the update prompt with a solid blue Update capsule and solid gray Later capsule; Later defers the same version for four hours and persists the deferral in app-config.json.

Render download progress as an island activity with a rotating blue left indicator and stable-width white right percentage, clamped monotonic progress, and unknown-length support; remove the standalone update progress/checking BrowserWindows and the native promptUpdateAvailable/promptUpdateDownloaded dialogs while keeping the skip-version capability (gap doc D3) and region-aware update sources (D2).

Schedule checks shortly after launch and every 24 hours alongside the existing manual check path; verify prompt precedence against music takeover, deferral across relaunch, 0-100% progress, failure recovery with manual retry, and install-on-quit behavior.

description: Honor the OS reduced-motion preference across all island widget motion, matching the macOS Reduce Motion behavior (gap G8).
steps: Observe prefers-reduced-motion in DynamicIslandWidget.tsx (framer-motion useReducedMotion or a media-query hook) and thread it through shell morphs, activity open/collapse, greeting entry, and hover transitions.
Provide visibly active non-animated fallbacks where motion carries meaning, including the update download indicator from win-update-prompt-and-download-in-island.
Verify with the Windows 'animation effects' setting toggled: reduced mode renders instant or minimal transitions with no layout breakage, and normal mode is unchanged.

description: Split the 1802-line main.cjs into focused modules with no behavior change.
steps: Extract updater + update-source/region logic, tray menu, widget window creation/anchoring, protocol (memoryflow://) handling, and config persistence into separate .cjs modules under front-end/electron/.
Keep main.cjs as composition-only wiring; preserve every IPC channel name, config key, and startup sequence exactly.
Verify electron:dev launches, widget loads #/widget, tray menu entries work, protocol callback still routes auth data, and a packaged build starts.

description: Prove the win-v* release pipeline end to end: tag, CI build, publish to MemoryFlow.exe, and auto-update from an older installed version.
steps: Push a win-v prerelease tag and verify release.yml validates the tag, syncs package.json version, builds the NSIS installer, and publishes to xixi131/MemoryFlow.exe.
Install the previous public release on a Windows machine, launch it, and verify it discovers and installs the new version through electron-updater, including the gh-proxy fallback path.
Record CI run, release asset, and update-flow evidence under docs/evidence/windows-release/, and confirm v* tags no longer trigger this workflow.
